comment: JSON-LD Schema Markup Examples for Google Images and Rich Results

projectSchema.name: Industrial Plant Modernization & Equipment Upgrade
projectSchema.description: Complete plant upgrade spanning 9 months from decommissioning through full operational commissioning in Trinidad and Tobago

imageSchemaExample.comment: Use this schema for EACH image in the gallery
imageSchemaExample.name: Existing Equipment Assessment - Determining Scope of Work
imageSchemaExample.alternativeText: Existing industrial crane and equipment frame showing aged infrastructure requiring modernization
imageSchemaExample.description: Initial site assessment of aging equipment and infrastructure to determine project scope. Shows weathered structural framework and lifting equipment that will be replaced or refurbished.
imageSchemaExample.keywords: equipment assessment, site inspection, industrial equipment, crane, structural frame

gallerySchema.comment: Wrap the entire project gallery with this schema
gallerySchema.name: Industrial Plant Modernization Project Gallery
gallerySchema.description: 31-image case study documenting a 9-month industrial plant upgrade from assessment through full commissioning

alt_text.description: Use alt text for ALL images - critical for SEO and accessibility
guidelines: Describe WHAT the image shows, not the action

visible_captions.description: Display captions below or alongside images for UX and SEO

structured_data.implementation: Add <script type="application/ld+json"> blocks to your HTML
structured_data.example:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "ImageObject",
  "name": "Existing Equipment Assessment - Determining Scope of Work",
  "url": "https://q2m.io/assets/projects/project-1/webp/01-IMG-20160109-WA0014.webp",
  "alternativeText": "Existing industrial crane and equipment frame showing aged infrastructure requiring modernization",
  "description": "Initial site assessment...",
  "datePublished": "2016-01-09"
}
</script>

complete_image_example.description: Full HTML example with all three components
